metadata.summary: Tool use as library or in cli to patch an APK, inject some libraries inside the APK or add a custom certificate

documentation:
# README


## GENERAL INFO


  Project: Library to patch apk (inject frida gadget)  
  Author: MadSquirrel  
  License: GNU General Public License v3.0  
  Version: v1.0  
  Date: 02-06-21  

## GOAL

Library to patch apk (inject frida gadget)
this code is inspired by this project :https://github.com/badadaf/apkpatcher.

The improvements added by this fork are the following:  
- modification of xml files such as AndroidManifest without extracting the resources. Extracting the resources usually prevents to rebuild the apk.
- Use as an API
- Installation as a package

## USAGE

  To use as library you just need to:

```python3
import apkpatcher
patcher = apkpatcher.Patcher(<apk_path>, <sdktools>, <version>)
patcher.patching(<path_gadget>, <arch>, output_file=<output_file>, user_certificate=<true|false>)
```

To use as a program you just need to:
```bash
apkpatcher -a <apk_path> -g <path_gadget> -s <sdktools> -b <version> -r <arch> -o <output_file>
```

For more information please visit https://apkpatcher.ci-yow.com/



## EXEMPLE

```python3
import apkpatcher
patcher = apkpatcher.Patcher(<apk_path>, <sdktools>, <version>)
# not mandatory
patcher.add_network_certificate(<custom_certificate>)
patcher.set_arch(<arch>)
patcher.pause(<True|False>)
# end not mandatory
patcher.patching(<path_gadget>, <arch>, output_file=<output_file>, user_certificate=<true|false>)
```

## INSTALL

```python3
sudo python3 -m pip install .
```

### Requirement
  setup your sktools as follow:
  - https://asthook.ci-yow.com/how.install.html#setup-sdktools
  install:
  - apktool
  - pip install -r requirements.txt


## CHANGELOG

functions.name: apkpatcher.smalipatching.get_smali_file_from_class
documentation: get the path of apk entrypoint on the smali files

functions.name: apkpatcher.smalipatching.replace_methods
documentation:
replace method in a smali content

Args:
    methods (list): list of method to target
    content (str): the smali content

Returns:
    str: return the new content

classes.name: apkpatcher.smalipatching.SmaliModifier
documentation: Public standard implementation of a Smali Source-Code writer.

modules.name: apkpatcher.conf
documentation: conf file

functions.name: apkpatcher.cli.main
documentation: cli function

modules.name: apkpatcher
documentation: !/usr/bin/python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.__init__
documentation:
Initialisation of patcher

Parameters:
            apk (str): path of the apk
            sdktools (str): path of the sdktools for zipalign
            version (str): version to choose the correct path of
            logger (logging.Logger) logger

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.add_network_certificate
documentation: CERTIFICATES                                                      #

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.create_security_config_xml
documentation:
Create security config file for add certificate

Args:
    path_network (str): path network

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.enable_user_certificates
documentation:
Enable user certificate

Args:
    rsc (pyaxml.ARSC): return ARSC file

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.inject_user_certificates_label
documentation:
Inject a proxy certificate directly inside the application

Args:
    rsc (pyaxml.ARSC): ARSC file (resource file of Android)

Raises:
    FileNotFoundError: raise when manifest is not found

Returns:
    str: return the path of network file

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.has_permission
documentation:
Check if the apk have 'permission_name' as permission

Parameters:
            permission_name (str): name of the permission with format:
            android.permission.XXX

Returns:
        has_permission (bool): permission is present
PERMISSIONS                                           #

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.inject_permission_manifest
documentation: Inject permission on the Manifest

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.extract_apk
documentation: Extract the apk on the temporary folder
EXTRACT REPACK APK                                            #

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.sign_and_zipalign
documentation: sign and zipalign file

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.pause
documentation:
enable a pause during the process to edit the application

Args:
    pause (bool): pause parameter

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.set_plugin
documentation:
set a plugin

Args:
    plugin (str | Path): _description_

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.repackage_apk
documentation:
repackage the apk

Parameters:
            - target_file (str) : the path of the new apk created if
              none, a new apk will be created with suffix "_patched.apk"

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.get_entrypoint_class_name
documentation: get the class name of the entrypoint
INJECT NATIVE CODE                                            #

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.get_entrypoint_smali_path
documentation: get the path of apk entrypoint on the smali files

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.insert_frida_loader
documentation: inject snippet to load frida-gadget in smali code

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.create_lib_arch_folders
documentation: make lib folder in the apk to put native lib

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.check_libextract
documentation:
check if extractNativeLibs is enable and active it

Returns:
    bool: return False if we didn't find manifest

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.insert_frida_lib
documentation:
Insert native lib inside the apk

Parameters:
            - gadget_path (str): the path of the gadget to insert

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.delete_existing_gadget
documentation: delete existing gadget inside the apk

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.set_arch
documentation:
set architecture of target phone where apk would be installed

Args:
    arch (str): architecture
PATCHING                                                      #

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.patching
documentation: patch the apk with gadget 'gadget_to_use'

methods.name: apkpatcher.Patcher.get_default_config_file
documentation: get a default config to frida to auto start frida at launch
